data.title: Update project docs for expanded onboarding config coverage
data.description:
## Description

Update existing documentation to reflect the expanded onboarding config parameter coverage after this epic completes. The doc-writer agent will receive the epic context and git diff to determine what needs updating.

### Files likely to modify
- CLAUDE.md — architecture section mentioning onboarding config generation (add new CI keys, version.file_path, stack)
- plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md — add entries for ci.fast_gate_job, ci.fast_fail_job, ci.test_ceil_job, ci.integration_workflow, version.file_path, stack; document merge.ci_workflow_name deprecation path

### Approach
- Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting and conventions
- Only update existing files — do not create new documentation files
- Describe the new CI Socratic dialogue UX flow (numbered selection when multiple workflows detected)
- Document the merge.ci_workflow_name → ci.workflow_name auto-migration
- Document version.file_path disambiguation (numbered selection for multiple version files)
- Document key name corrections: jira.project (not jira.project_key), design.system_name (not design.system)

### Unit test exemption justification
1. No conditional logic — purely documentation content updates
2. Any test would be a change-detector (grep for specific doc text)
3. Infrastructure-boundary-only — documentation layer, no business logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLAUDE.md architecture section mentions new onboarding config keys if applicable
  Verify: grep -q "ci.fast_gate_job\|ci.integration_workflow\|version.file_path" CLAUDE.md || echo "Not referenced in CLAUDE.md (may be correct if not architecture-relevant)"
- [ ] CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md includes entries for new config keys
  Verify: grep -q "ci.fast_gate_job" plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md
- [ ] merge.ci_workflow_name deprecation is documented
  Verify: grep -q "merge.ci_workflow_name" plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md
- [ ] No new documentation files created (only existing files updated)
  Verify: test -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=A HEAD)"
